page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an arm64 architecture utilizing a dedicated Ampere Altra processor running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 physical cores with 4 vCPUs, providing a single thread per core, and is paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests register average performance levels, with scores of 1789.99 and 7136.84 respectively. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds of 50.64 GB/sec and 29.82 GB/sec, though small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak at 283.98 GB/sec. Passmark CPU tests reveal weaker performance in extended instructions and physics, with a poor rating in encryption tests. However, database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, with Redis achieving 5.78 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6r.xlarge offers a balanced resource density for arm64-native applications without the overhead of bundled local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance, making it suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, lightweight database operations, and basic text or language model inference tasks that do not require high-performance cryptography or specialized vector instructions.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an arm64-based Ampere Altra processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show average results for stress-ng CPU tests and uncached memory bandwidth, alongside average performance in Redis SET operations and database tasks. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for arm64-native applications, making it suitable for general-purpose workloads, lightweight databases, and web serving.

tagline: An arm64-based general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Ampere Altra cores and balanced memory allocation for standard cloud workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage
Average performance in Redis operations and large-block memory bandwidth
